message:
refactor(ffs-extent): return InvalidGeometry from split_for_mark_written and consolidate test search

Follow-up to cea5c9b (reject logical ranges that exceed ext4's 32-bit
block space). That commit widened the logical arithmetic to u64 and
added `u32::try_from(mark_end).expect(...)` at the two right-hand
split insertion points. The expect invariant is enforced by callers
today, but propagating a typed error is more defensive — malformed
split inputs from a future caller would otherwise panic in a library
used on user-controlled filesystem images.

ffs-extent/src/lib.rs — public behaviour:

- split_for_mark_written now returns Result<Vec<Ext4Extent>>. Both
  right-hand split branches (span-entire-range and start-within-range)
  convert u32::try_from errors into FfsError::InvalidGeometry with
  the offending mark_end value in the detail string, and the function
  returns Ok(out) on the success path.
- mark_written call site updated to propagate the error with `?`.
- ExtentCache.invalidate_range's unchecked `expect` on the clamped
  u32::try_from is replaced with `.unwrap_or(u32::MAX)` since the
  branch is explicitly defensive (we've already saturating_add'd to
  u64 before the clamp, so the try_from failure case is benign and
  the fallback to u32::MAX correctly widens the invalidation range).

ffs-extent/src/lib.rs — tests:

- Add a `found_extent(cx, dev, root, logical_block) -> Result<Option<Ext4Extent>>`
  helper that does an ffs_btree::search and returns Some(extent) for
  SearchResult::Found and None for SearchResult::Hole. Lifted from
  the duplicated match arms that were peppered through the test
  module.
- Rewrite all test-side search+match scaffolding (mark_written,
  truncate, split-range, mark_written_straddling_extents, and the
  Corruption-path assertions) to use `found_extent(...).expect(...)`
  and `result.expect_err(...)` + `assert!(matches!(...))`. Removes
  ~50 lines of repeated `match {...} => panic!(...)` boilerplate
  without changing any assertion semantics.
- Existing split_for_mark_written unit tests updated to unwrap the
  new Result return.

The contract change is intentional: callers of
split_for_mark_written are now compile-required to handle an
InvalidGeometry error instead of relying on an undocumented panic
path. Test coverage for the error path remains implicit (the
invariant is upheld by mark_written's range_end clamping), so no new
assertion is added for the error surface itself.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
